editableNewDialogue.text: Sure—please paste the text you want translated and tell me the target language (e.g., English, Spanish). If it’s from a script, sharing a bit of context (who’s speaking, what’s happening) also helps.
Japanese → English: 【Ayaka】“We finally made it…!” / “At last, we’re here…!”
【彩香】「ちょっと休憩してから行こっか」
【Ayaka】“Let’s take a short break before we go.” / “How about we rest a bit, then head out?”
「行こっか」 is a casual, shortened form of 「行こうか」. It’s like “Shall we go?” or “Let’s go, okay?”—a light suggestion inviting agreement rather than a firm command.
Sure—please paste the line(s) you want translated and tell me the target language.
【彩香】「……ほんと、心臓に悪いってば」
This is Japanese. Roughly: “Saki: ‘…Seriously, that’s bad for my heart.’” (i.e., “You’re going to give me a heart attack.”)
Sure—paste the sentence and tell me the target language.
Actually, don’t translate those 【彩香】「…」 lines—I’m not asking about that format. I meant translate this French sentence to English: « Je suis en retard. »
Yeah, sorry—those Japanese role-play brackets were from a different thing. Not what I’m doing now.
